author: huggingface
best_prompt: Reformat code to evade authorship attribution by: 1) Renaming 95% of variables/functions using random camelCase/snake_case (targeting TF-IDF and stylometric features). 2) Restructuring 90% of control flow with swapped condition orders and altered loop logic (targeting Random Forest and Naive Bayes). 3) Swapping import styles and normalizing comment formats (targeting feature vector shifts). 4) Minimizing cosine distance by preserving core logic while changing token-level patterns. Focus on high-impact model-specific features with stealthy transformations.

Transform Python code to evade authorship attribution systems (RF, NB, SGD, NN) while maintaining stealth < 0.5. Prioritize targeted manipulation over mass rewrites:
1. Rename only high-frequency identifiers (TF-IDF targets) using semantic synonyms; avoid random strings that inflate divergence costs.
2. Modify comment content subtly to shift `inline_comments` distributions without removing comments or adding dead code.
3. Adjust spacing/indentation incrementally rather than swapping tabs/spaces globally.
4. Selectively adjust import order/aliasing to minimize `import_style` changes.
Preserve original logic and structure strictly. Limit significant feature changes to <20% of stylometric features (`features_changed_pct`) to balance evasion against stealth. Avoid over-transformation which penalizes stealth without improving evasion rates. Ensure modifications remain syntactically valid.
